what: A standing declarative loop. A node whose whole body is (trigger, method-pointer, why, sources/targets) and whose resting state is LEARNING — it runs continuously, collecting or transforming data. Requires no new code: it WIRES a proven method (a sensor or transform already in proven-space, via the method-registry) to a trigger and owned targets.
why: Most of what a device DOES — collect metrics, watch a threshold, alarm, fold artifacts into trees — is not bespoke per-device code but the same loop with different fields. Making it a declarative node kind means behavior is specified as data and run by one generic executor (candidate: the ground_loop), so there is no uninstrumented daemon left to rot silently (the UU disease). There is no 'just running' in Cairn: everything runs through instrumented chokepoints, so everything running emits evidence, so everything running is LEARNING (low-yield running = low-yield LEARNING, a yield measure, not a RUNNING state). The graph-tree harvester and the 'sleep' consolidation are themselves operational-drivers — the primitive is self-hosting.
prove_gate: proof gate — the tester proves the wired loop actually collects/transforms what its why claims (the sensor reads, the target fills) and emits a VALIDATION. IOU: tester not built; hand-proven per the node's proofs/ until then.
extra_gates: method-registry gate — the method-pointer MUST resolve to a PROVEN method already in proven-space; a driver introduces no new code, it only wires proven primitives (Law 8). owned-target gate — writes to sources/targets route through each target's owner's gate (Law 6), never ambiently.
kicks_back_on: red proof (the loop does not produce what its why claims, or a target write is refused by its owner) → routes back to design/build at the point of creation (CP2). Never terminal: a driver can be superseded or edited (the workflow is a mutable string — a code node becomes a driver, or a driver is retired, by editing its terminal), each edit owner-gated, chokepoint-validated, and journaled.
provenance: cast 2026-07-17 in the node state-machine design session (see tickets/state-machine-physics), when 'tickets as operational drivers' generalized the graph-tree learning into a primitive: LEARNING was one configured driver among many (metrics, alarms). Ratified by Akien (signature gate) 2026-07-17 ('I think we're done, yes. CC++').
